# [OS-03] Contributor / triage funnel

> Plan ID `OS-03` — local plan identifier in this issue pack, **not** a
> GitHub issue number. See [`00_EPIC_2026.md`](00_EPIC_2026.md).

## Status

🟡 **Partial.** CI is public and gates `iverilog` elaboration + R-SI-1
+ canonical anchor. A contributor doc (`CONTRIBUTING.md`, triage
labels, RFC template) is **not yet committed**.

## Plan

1. Land `CONTRIBUTING.md` covering: how to clone, how to run the
   sim suite, how to run R-SI-1 locally, how to attach an RTL
   change to a witness crate, and how to file an RFC for a new
   sacred opcode.
2. Add an RFC template under `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` (or
   `.github/RFC_TEMPLATE.md`) modelled on the plan-item structure
   used in this issue pack.
3. Define and apply the label set used by this issue pack:
   `track:clara`, `track:energy`, `track:snn-tri`,
   `track:publication`, `track:open-source`, `gate:rtl`,
   `gate:sim`, `gate:formal`, `gate:bench`, `status:done`,
   `status:partial`, `status:planned`, `status:target`,
   `status:gated`, `chip:gamma`, `epic`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `CONTRIBUTING.md` committed.
- [ ] RFC template committed under `.github/`.
- [ ] Label set defined in the repo (manual or via
      `.github/labels.yml`).
- [ ] `create_issues.sh` runs cleanly against the defined labels.

## R5 honesty

This issue is about **process artefacts**, not licensing or
governance. It does not attempt to bind contributors to a CLA or
sign-off policy beyond what is already in `LICENSE`.
